tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: teammate_A is ditching me again, but since I care more about them than the project, I'll just do their work to keep things smooth.

desires: Finish teammate_A's work immediately so we can grab lunch with cohort_3 and avoid the awkwardness of them ditching me again.
Protect my relationship with teammate_A by absorbing their failure, even though it means I'm ignoring my own need for sleep tonight.
Avoid any confrontation or boundary-setting right now because arguing feels like too much effort when I'm already stressed.

goals.description: Immediately take over teammate_A's unfinished project share to save the group dynamic and secure lunch with cohort_3.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my #1 priority (0.5). I cannot risk the awkwardness of being left out by cohort_3 again, and I'm too impulsive to negotiate a boundary right now when I just want to fix this mess so we can all hang out.

goals.description: Ignore my own sleep debt and finish both parts of the project before 7 PM, even if it means staying up late.
goals.rationale: My planning horizon is short; I'm discounting the future consequences of exhaustion because the immediate social payoff (lunch + avoiding conflict) feels more urgent than my health (0.13 value).

goals.description: Do not tell teammate_A that their request was unreasonable or set a boundary about workload distribution.
goals.rationale: I am currently stressed and low on energy. Confrontation feels like too much effort, and I'd rather absorb the failure to keep the peace, even though I usually assert myself.
